questions.question: Explain the one-way ANOVA procedure. Derive the partitioning of total sum of squares into between-groups and within-groups components. Construct the ANOVA table.
keyPoints: Definition and purpose of one-way ANOVA
Degrees of freedom for each component

questions.question: The following data represent the scores of students taught by three different methods. Method A: 85, 78, 82, 80, 75. Method B: 90, 88, 95, 92, 85. Method C: 70, 65, 72, 68, 75. Perform a one-way ANOVA at the 5% level of significance to test whether the methods differ significantly.
keyPoints: Compute group means and grand mean
Construct ANOVA table with MS and F values
Compare with F-critical and state conclusion

questions.question: State the assumptions of one-way ANOVA. Explain the F-test and decision rule used in one-way ANOVA with an example.
keyPoints: Three assumptions: independence, normality, homogeneity of variances
F-statistic as ratio of MSB to MSW
F-distribution with (k-1, N-k) degrees of freedom

questions.question: Four types of fertilizers are tested for their effect on crop yield. Fertilizer 1: 23, 25, 20. Fertilizer 2: 28, 30, 25. Fertilizer 3: 20, 18, 22. Fertilizer 4: 25, 27, 23. Perform one-way ANOVA at alpha = 0.05.
keyPoints: Calculate group means and grand mean
Compute SSB and SSW using correction factor method

questions.question: Explain the correction factor method for computing sums of squares in one-way ANOVA. Illustrate with a suitable example.
